TRIAL MOTION IN DEPT. 220, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL PURSUANT TO CCP 36(A) IS TRANSFERRED TO THE ASBESTOS DEPARTMENT, DEPT. 220, FOR THE MOTION TO BE HEARD BEFORE JUDGE KAHN. THERE BEING NO OBJECTION TO THE TENTATIVE RULING, THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR PREFERENCE IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO CCP 36(A). THE COURT ORDERS PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER ON THE TRIAL DATE AND THE OBLIGATIONS ON ALL PARTIES RELATING TO THE PREFERENCE ORDER. AFTER GOING OFF RECORD, THE COURT CONTINUES PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR PREFERENCE TO MAR-15-2010, 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 220 ON THE ISSUE WHEN TRIAL DATE IS SET AND ANY COMPONENTS OF THE PREFERENCE ORDER. PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION MAY BE FILED ALONG WITH ANY FURTHER OPPOSITIONS BY 4:30PM ON 3/12. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; CLERK: D. LOK; REPORTER: KIMBER JENNINGS, CSR #11452 (220)
MINI-MINUTES FOR MAR-15-2010 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 220

ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANT PLANT PRODUCTS & SUPPLY COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT PLANT PRODUCTS & SUPPLY COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT PLANT PRODUCTS & SUPPLY COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SUSTAINED ITS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION AND PLAINTIFFS FAILED TO PRESENT EVIDENCE CREATING A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER THE INSULATION TO WHICH MR. GVOZDENOVIC WAS EXPOSED BY M. SLAYEN EMPLOYEES WAS SOLD, SUPPLIED OR DISTRIBUTED BY DEFENDANT. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO THE DEPOSITION TESTIMONY OF MR. SLAYEN ARE SUSTAINED. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO DECEDENT'S DECLARATION ARE OVERRULED AS TO ITS ADMISSIBILITY AS A DYING DECLARATION AND SUSTAINED AS LACKING FOUNDATION AND SPECULATIVE ONLY AS TO THE STATEMENTS THAT THE INSULATION CONTAINED ASBESTOS. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO MR. CASH'S DECLARATION ARE OVERRULED AS TO ITS OVERALL ADMISSIBILITY AND SUSTAINED AS LACKING FOUNDATION AND SPECULATIVE ONLY AS TO THE STATEMENTS THAT THE INSULATION CONTAINED ASBESTOS. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Dec-15-2011 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Dec-20-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
David F. Beach, Esq., of BISHOP, BARRY & DRATH (510-596-0888), appearing for defendant / moving party Plant Products & Supply Company
Steven J. Patti, Esq., of CLAPPER, PATTI, SCHWEIZER & MASON (415-332-4262), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Ziva Gvozdenovic, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT PLANT PRODUCTS & SUPPLY COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Plant Products & Supply Company's motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether the insulation to which Mr. Gvozdenovic was exposed by M. Slayen employees was sold, supplied or distributed by defendant. Defendant's objections to the deposition testimony of Mr. Slayen are sustained. Defendant's objections to decedent's declaration are overruled as to its admissibility as a dying declaration and sustained as lacking foundation and speculative only as to the statements that the insulation contained asbestos. Defendant's objections to Mr. Cash's declaration are overruled as to its overall admissibility and sustained as lacking foundation and speculative only as to the statements that the insulation contained asbestos. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Leland Batara, CSR # 3759 = (503/TLJ)
